LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIMEON MASON IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS DEMURRER IS IDENTICAL TO THE DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT FILED IN PARR V. FORD AND WILL BE DECIDED IDENTICALLY. IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, MR. MASON HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS TO AVOID THE 3-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OF CCP 338. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna H. Cook of the law offices of Strategic Legal Practices APC (310.929.4900) appears via telephone as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Micah Yospe of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415.986.5900) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Anna H. Cook of the law offices of Strategic Legal Practices APC (310.929.4900) appears via telephone as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Micah Yospe of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415.986.5900) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to the sixth cause of action for fraud in the complaint filed by plaintiff Simeon Mason is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend. This demurrer is identical to the demurrer to the complaint filed in Parr v. Ford and will be decided identically. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Mason has leave to plead facts to avoid the 3-year statute of limitations of CCP 338. Paragraph 8 of the complaint alleges that Mr. Mason purchased the vehicle on September 1, 2012 and he filed this action more than 3 years later on November 1, 2016. Mr. Mason's allegations seeking to invoke tolling and delayed discovery doctrines are conclusory and lacking any ultimate facts. Mr. Mason needs to allege facts to support his theories to avoid the statute of limitations. (Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 437; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808; McDonald v Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008) 45 Cal.4th 88, 102; Baker v. Beech Aircraft Corp. (1974) 39 Cal.App.3d 315, 321.) Ford's remaining arguments lack merit. Less particularity is required for pleading a fraud claim when the facts lie within Ford's knowledge. (Alfaro v. Comm. Housing Imp. Systems & Planning Ass'n (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) Paragraphs 39 and 42 of the complaint support a fraud theory of liability based on Ford's exclusive knowledge of the defects. "[W]here one party to a transaction has sole knowledge or access to material facts and knows that such facts are not known or reasonably discoverable by the other party, then a duty to disclose exists." (Shapiro v. Sutherland (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1534, 1544.) Paragraph 43 of the complaint also alleges a theory of recovery based on active concealment. Ford can conduct discovery to determine the scope of Mr. Mason's damages. The economic loss rule is inapplicable if Mr. Mason is able to plead a non-time barred fraud claim. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
